name: Little Richard
abstract: Richard Wayne Penniman (December 5, 1932 – May 9, 2020), better known by his stage name Little Richard, was an American singer-songwriter and pianist. He was an influential figure in popular music and culture for seven decades.

about_quotes.speaker: Dan D'Imperio
about_quotes.evidence: In that band was Carter Jefferson... But he didn't know anything about — he had been with Little Richard and Jackie Wilson, bands like that.

about_quotes.speaker: Hentoff Nat
about_quotes.evidence: You’re born 1925, so you’re already 30 years old, Bill Haley and the Comets and Elvis and Little Richard and Fats Domino and all that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hentoff Nat — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Milton Batiste
about_quotes.evidence: We joined that band. To play behind the Famous Flames. With the lead singer was James Brown. And the person who was in the head of the show. The top of the show. Was Richard Pennyworth. Yeah. Pennyworth. Yeah. You know what I'm saying? Little Richard. Little Richard. So. We worked a couple of gigs a

about_quotes.speaker: Tommy Ridgley
about_quotes.evidence: Little Richard and I were fairly good friends. He asked me to go on the road with him.

about_quotes.speaker: Alvin "Red" Tyler
about_quotes.evidence: I went on the road for a short period of time with little Richard

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee discusses his brother Richard extensively. The mention of 'Little Richard' occurs only when the interviewer references Gil Evans seeing a Little Richard record in the interviewer's collection.
